title.zh: 卷三十四 梁統列傳
title.raw: 卷三十四 梁統列傳 Volume 34: Biography of Liang Tong

sentences.zh: 梁統字仲寧，安定烏氏人，晉大夫梁益耳，即其先也。

translations.literal: Liang Tong, courtesy name Zhongning, was a man of Wushi in Anding; the Jin noble Liang Yier was his ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Tong, styled Zhongning, came from Wushi in Anding; his line descended from the Jin minister Liang Yier.

sentences.zh: 統高祖父子都，自河東遷居北地，子都子橋，以資千萬徙茂陵，至哀、平之末，歸安定。

translations.literal: Tong's great-grandfather Zidu moved from Hedong to reside in Beidi; Zidu's son Qiao, with assets of ten million cash, moved to Maoling; reaching the end of the Ai and Ping reigns, they returned to Anding.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather Liang Zidu had relocated from Hedong to Beidi; Zidu's son Liang Qiao took a fortune of ten million to settle at Maoling, then the family returned to Anding late in the Ai and Ping era.

sentences.zh: 統性剛毅而好法律。

translations.literal: Tong's nature was firm and resolute and he loved statutes.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Tong was stern by temperament and devoted to the law.

sentences.zh: 初仕州郡。

translations.literal: At first he served in province and commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He began his career in provincial and commandery office.

sentences.zh: 更始二年，召補中郎將，使安集涼州，拜酒泉太守。

translations.literal: In the second year of Gengshi he was summoned and supplemented as general of the gentlemen of the palace, sent to pacify and gather Liangzhou, appointed governor of Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: In Gengshi 2 he was named general of the gentlemen of the palace, dispatched to settle Liangzhou, and made governor of Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 會更始敗，赤眉入長安，統與竇融及諸郡守起兵保境，謀共立帥。

translations.literal: When Gengshi failed, the Red Eyebrows entered Chang'an; Tong with Dou Rong and various commandery governors raised troops to guard the borders, plotted together to establish a leader.
translations.idiomatic: When Gengshi fell and the Red Eyebrows took Chang'an, Liang Tong joined Dou Rong and the frontier governors in mobilizing to defend their territory and choose a leader.

sentences.zh: 初以位次，咸共推統，統固辭曰：『昔陳嬰不受王者，以有老母也。

translations.literal: At first by order of rank all jointly pushed Tong; Tong firmly declined saying: 'Formerly Chen Ying did not accept kingship because he had an aged mother.
translations.idiomatic: At first they pressed Liang Tong by seniority, but he refused: 'Chen Ying once declined a throne because his mother was old.'

sentences.zh: 今統內有尊親，又德薄能寡，誠不足以當之。

translations.literal: Now Tong within has honored kin; moreover virtue thin ability few—truly not sufficient to undertake it.
translations.idiomatic: I still have elders at home and mean talent—I am not fit for such a burden.'

sentences.zh: 』遂共推融為河西大將軍，更以統為武威大守。

translations.literal: ' Then jointly they pushed Rong as Grand General west of the river; changed and made Tong governor of Wuwei.
translations.idiomatic: ' They chose Dou Rong as Grand General of Hexi and named Liang Tong governor of Wuwei.

sentences.zh: 為政嚴猛，威行鄰郡。

translations.literal: In governing he was stern and fierce; awe extended neighboring commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: He ruled with severity; neighboring commanderies felt his authority.

sentences.zh: 建武五年，統籌各遣使隨竇融長史劉鈞詣闕奉貢，願得詣行在所，詔加統宣德將軍。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Jianwu, Tong planned each sending envoys following Dou Rong's chief clerk Liu Jun to the palace gate presenting tribute, wished able to go to the traveling headquarters; edict added Tong General Who Spreads Virtue.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 5 Liang Tong arranged for each commandery to send envoys with Dou Rong's chief clerk Liu Jun to present tribute at court and seek audience with the emperor, who then named Liang Tong General Who Spreads Virtue.

sentences.zh: 八年夏，光武自征隗囂，統與竇融等將兵會車駕。

translations.literal: In summer of the eighth year, Guangwu personally campaigned against Wei Xiao; Tong with Dou Rong and others led troops and joined the imperial carriage.
translations.idiomatic: In summer of year 8 Guangwu marched against Wei Xiao; Liang Tong and Dou Rong brought their armies to join him.

sentences.zh: 及囂敗，封統為成義侯，同產兄巡、從弟騰並為關內侯，拜騰酒泉典農部尉，悉遣還河西。

translations.literal: When Xiao was defeated, Tong was enfeoffed marquis of Chengyi; full-blood elder brother Xun and cousin younger brother Teng alike became interior marquises; Teng was appointed agricultural colonel of Jiuquan; all were sent back west of the river.
translations.idiomatic: After Wei Xiao fell, Liang Tong was invested as Marquis of Chengyi; his brother Liang Xun and cousin Liang Teng became interior marquises; Teng was named agricultural colonel of Jiuquan, and all returned to Hexi.

sentences.zh: 十二年，統與融等俱詣京師，以列侯奉朝請，更封高山侯，拜太中大夫，除四子為郎。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, Tong with Rong and others altogether went to the capital; as ranked marquis attended court audience; transferred enfeoffment marquis of Gaoshan; appointed grand counselor of the palace; appointed four sons gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: In year 12 Liang Tong accompanied Dou Rong to the capital as a marquis at court, was reinvested as Marquis of Gaoshan, named grand counselor of the palace, and had four sons appointed gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 統在朝廷，數陳便宜。

translations.literal: Tong at court repeatedly stated what was advantageous.
translations.idiomatic: At court Liang Tong often urged timely reforms.

sentences.zh: 以為法令既輕，下奸不勝。

translations.literal: He held that statutes already light, inferior villainy could not be borne.
translations.idiomatic: He argued that with laws too lenient, villainy below could not be checked.

sentences.zh: 宜重刑罰，以遵舊典，乃上疏曰：

translations.literal: It was fitting to heavy punishments, to follow old canons; then he presented a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Punishments should be stiffened to match ancient precedent—he therefore memorialized:

sentences.zh: 臣竊見元、哀二帝輕殊死之刑以一百二十三事，手殺人者減死一等，自是以後，著為常準，故人輕犯法，吏易殺人。

translations.literal: Your servant has privately seen Emperors Yuan and Ai lighten capital punishment in one hundred twenty-three matters; those who killed with their own hand had death reduced one grade; from this afterward recorded as constant norm; therefore people lightly violated law; clerks easily killed men.
translations.idiomatic: I note that under Yuandi and Aidi capital crimes were relaxed in 123 provisions—manslaughter dropped one degree below death—and that precedent invited petty crime and quick executions by officials.

sentences.zh: 臣聞立君之道，仁義為主，仁者愛人，義者政理，愛人以除殘為務，政理以去亂為心。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that establishing a lord's way takes humanity and rightness as lord; the humane love men; the righteous regulate governance; loving men takes removing cruelty as task; regulating governance takes removing chaos as heart.
translations.idiomatic: The ruler's way rests on humanity and justice: humanity cherishes the people and crusades against cruelty; justice orders the realm and aims at ending chaos.

sentences.zh: 刑罰在衷，無取於勸，是以五帝有流、殛、放、殺之誅，三王有大辟、刻肌之法。

translations.literal: Punishments lie in the middle—nothing taken from exhortation—therefore the Five Thearchs had exile, spear-execution, banishment, and killing punishments; the Three Kings had great execution and cutting flesh laws.
translations.idiomatic: Penalties must strike the balance—no empty preaching—and so the Five Thearchs knew exile and execution, while the Three Dynasties relied on capital punishment and branding.

sentences.zh: 故孔子稱『仁者必有勇』，又曰『理財正辭，禁民為非曰義』。

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius praised 'The humane must have courage,' and also said 'ordering wealth and correcting words, forbidding the people wrongdoing is called righteousness.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said the humane are brave, and that ordering livelihoods, correcting speech, and barring wrongdoing is righteousness.

sentences.zh: 高帝受命誅暴，平蕩天下，約令定律，誠得其宜。

translations.literal: Gaodi received mandate executed cruelty, leveled and swept the realm, covenant ordered fixing statutes—truly obtained its fitness.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu swept away tyranny and framed statutes that fit the age.

sentences.zh: 文帝寬惠柔克，遭世康平，惟除省肉刑、相坐之法，它皆率由，無革舊章。

translations.literal: Wendi was generous and mild subduing; encountered age peace and calm; only eliminated reduced flesh punishment and linked-guilt laws; other all followed along; none altered old regulations.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen ruled gently in tranquil times, trimming only the mutilating punishments and clan liability—otherwise he followed Han precedent untouched.

sentences.zh: 武帝值中國隆盛，財力有餘，征伐遠方，軍役數興，豪桀犯禁，奸吏弄法，故重首匿之科，著知從之律，以破朋黨，以懲隱匿。

translations.literal: Wudi encountered Central States lofty flourishing; wealth power had surplus; campaigned attacked distant regions; military service repeatedly arose; heroes violated prohibitions; villainous clerks played with law; therefore heavy chief concealing categories, established knowing-follow statutes, to break faction parties, to punish concealment.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu flourished with surplus revenue and constant campaigns; bold men broke the law and corrupt clerks twisted it—so he tightened harboring statutes and accessory guilt to smash factions and punish concealment.

sentences.zh: 宣帝聰明正直，總禦海內，臣下奉憲，無所失墜，因循先典，天下稱理。

translations.literal: Xuandi clever and upright, overall commanded within seas; ministers below upheld statutes, none missed fall; followed along former canons; realm called ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan was astute and upright: officials obeyed the code, precedent held, and the realm was deemed well governed.

sentences.zh: 至哀、平繼體，而即位日淺，聽斷尚寡，丞相王嘉輕為穿鑿，虧除先帝舊約成律，數年之間，百有餘事，或不便於理，或不厭民心。

translations.literal: Reaching Ai and Ping succeeding the body, yet enthronement days shallow, hearing judgments still few; Chancellor Wang Jia lightly made boring-through; harmed removed former emperors' old compacts completed statutes; within several years, more than hundred matters; some not convenient to pattern; some not satisfying people's hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Under Aidi and Pingdi—short reigns and thin court experience—Chancellor Wang Jia rashly chipped away at statutes Gaodi had settled; in a few years over a hundred changes either offended reason or the people.

sentences.zh: 謹表其尤害於體者傅奏於左。

translations.literal: Respectfully listing those especially harmful to the body attach memorial at left.
translations.idiomatic: I append the worst of these—those that damage good rule—to the left.

sentences.zh: 伏惟陛下包元履德，權時撥亂，功逾文、武，德侔高皇，誠不宜因循季末衰微之軌。

translations.literal: Prostrate I consider Your Majesty embraces origin treads virtue, weighs times removes chaos; merit surpasses Wen and Wu; virtue matches Gaodi—truly ought not follow late-age declining faint tracks.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty restored order with virtue surpassing Kings Wen and Wu—you must not cling to the dying expedients of the late Han.

sentences.zh: 回神明察，考量得失，宣詔有司，詳擇其善，定不易之典，施無窮之法，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: Turn divine brightness examine; examine measure gain loss; proclaim edict responsible offices; detailed choose their good; fix unchanging canons; apply inexhaustible laws—realm greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: May Your Majesty weigh right and wrong, instruct the offices to choose the best, fix enduring statutes, and lay down lasting law—the empire would rejoice.

sentences.zh: 事下三公、廷尉，議者以為隆刑竣法，非明王急務，施行日久，豈一朝所厘。

translations.literal: The matter went down to Three Dukes and commandant of justice; debaters held that severe punishments and harsh laws were not a bright king's urgent task; implemented long—how in one morning to rectify.
translations.idiomatic: The matter went to the Three Dukes and the commandant of justice; critics held harsh laws were no enlightened ruler's first priority and that long usage could not be revised overnight.

sentences.zh: 統今所定，不宜開可。

translations.literal: What Tong now has determined ought not be opened and approved.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Tong's proposal should not be adopted.

sentences.zh: 統復上言曰：『有司以臣今所言，不可施行。

translations.literal: Tong again submitted speech saying: 'Responsible offices hold that what your servant now speaks cannot be implemented.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Tong wrote again: 'The ministries say my proposals cannot be carried out.'

sentences.zh: 尋臣之所奏，非曰嚴刑。

translations.literal: Trace what your servant has memorialized—it is not called severe punishments.
translations.idiomatic: Consider my point: I am not merely crying for cruelty.

sentences.zh: 竊謂高帝以後，至乎孝宣，其所施行，多合經傳，宜比方今事，驗之往古，聿遵前典，事無難改，不勝至願。

translations.literal: Privately I hold from Gaodi onward, reaching to Xiaoxuan, what they implemented mostly matched classics and commentaries; it is fitting to compare square today's affairs, verify them in ancient past, then follow former canons—affairs are not hard to change—unsurpassed utmost wish.
translations.idiomatic: From Gaozu through Xuandi the code matched the classics; compare present cases to antiquity, restore sound precedent—such reform is no great hurdle and would fulfill my deepest hope.

sentences.zh: 願得召見，若對尚書近臣，口陳其要。

translations.literal: I wish to obtain summons and audience; if facing Masters of Writing close ministers, orally state its essentials.
translations.idiomatic: Let me face the throne—or at least the Masters of Writing—and state the essentials in person.'

sentences.zh: 』帝令尚書問狀，統對曰：

translations.literal: ' The emperor ordered the Masters of Writing to ask the situation; Tong replied saying:
translations.idiomatic: ' The emperor had the Masters of Writing question him, and Liang Tong answered:

sentences.zh: 聞聖帝明王，制立刑罰，故雖堯、舜之盛，猶誅四凶。

translations.literal: I have heard sage emperors and bright kings established punishments; therefore though Yao and Shun's flourishing, still executed the Four Villains.
translations.idiomatic: Sage kings always frame punishments—even Yao and Shun put down the Four Villains.

sentences.zh: 經曰：『天討有罪，五刑五庸哉。

translations.literal: The classic says: 'Heaven punishes guilty; the five punishments, five services.'
translations.idiomatic: The classic says, 'Heaven chastises the guilty and ordains the five punishments to fit five kinds of service.'

sentences.zh: 』又曰：『爰制百姓於刑之衷。

translations.literal: ' It also says: 'Thus restrain the hundred surnames in the middle of punishments.'
translations.idiomatic: ' It adds, 'Hold the people to the even measure of the law.'

sentences.zh: 』孔子曰：『刑罰不衷，則人無所厝手足。

translations.literal: ' Confucius said: 'When punishments are not centered, then people have nowhere to place hands and feet.'
translations.idiomatic: ' Confucius said, 'When penalties miss the mean, people do not know where to stand.'

sentences.zh: 』衷之為言，不輕不重之謂也。

translations.literal: ' As for "centered," it speaks of neither light nor heavy.
translations.idiomatic: ' "The mean" means neither too harsh nor too lenient.'

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之誅，不避親戚，所以防患救亂，全安眾庶，豈無仁愛之恩？

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn's executions did not avoid kin; by this prevented harm and saved chaos, wholly secured the multitude—how could there be no benevolent loving grace?
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn executed kin when needed to forestall disaster—was that not mercy to the many?

sentences.zh: 貴絕殘賊之路也。

translations.literal: It valued cutting off the road of cruelty and banditry.
translations.idiomatic: It shut the door on brutality and theft.

sentences.zh: 自高祖之興，至於孝宣，君明臣忠，謨謀深博，猶因循舊章，不輕改革，海內稱理，斷獄益少。

translations.literal: From Gaozu's rise to Xiaoxuan, lord bright ministers loyal, plans deep and broad; still followed old regulations, did not lightly reform; within seas called ordered; judged cases increasingly few.
translations.idiomatic: From Gaozu to Xuandi, wise rulers and loyal ministers kept established law and seldom altered it—the realm was deemed orderly and lawsuits dwindled.

sentences.zh: 至初元、建平，所減刑罰百有餘條，而盜賊浸多，歲以萬數。

translations.literal: Reaching Chuyuan and Jianping, reduced punishments more than hundred articles; yet robbers and bandits gradually many, yearly by tens of thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Under Chuyuan and Jianping over a hundred mitigations piled up—yet bandits multiplied by tens of thousands each year.

sentences.zh: 間者三輔從橫，群輩並起，至燔燒茂陵，火見未央。

translations.literal: Recently the Three Adjuncts ran rampant; crowds arose together; reaching burning Maoling; fire appeared Weiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Lately the capital region erupted—bands ran riot until Maoling burned and flames lit Weiyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 其後隴西、北地、西河之賊，越州度郡，萬里交結，攻取庫兵，劫略吏人，詔書討捕，連年不獲。

translations.literal: Afterward Longxi, Beidi, Xihe bandits crossed provinces passed commanderies, ten thousand li linked; attacked took armory weapons; robbed plundered clerks and people; edict script punish capture; successive years not caught.
translations.idiomatic: Then Longxi, Beidi, and Xihe rebels ranged across provinces, seized arsenals, looted officials and commoners, and defied years of imperial hunts.

sentences.zh: 是時以天下無難，百姓安平，而狂狡之勢，猶至於此，皆刑罰不衷，愚人易犯之所致也。

translations.literal: At this time though the realm had no hardship, hundred surnames peaceful and calm; yet wild and crafty momentum still reached thus—all punishments not centered, foolish people easily offending caused.
translations.idiomatic: Though the age seemed calm, villainy still exploded—that stems from penalties missing the mean and inviting petty crime.

sentences.zh: 由此觀之，則刑輕之作，反生大患；

translations.literal: From this viewing it, then light punishments' making, contrary births great calamity;
translations.idiomatic: Lenient statutes breed greater harm.

sentences.zh: 惠加奸軌，而害及良善也。

translations.literal: kindness added to villainous tracks, while harm reached good and kind.
translations.idiomatic: Lenient laws indulged villains and hurt the good.

sentences.zh: 故臣統願陛下采擇賢臣孔光、師丹等議。

translations.literal: Therefore your servant Tong wishes Your Majesty adopt choose worthy ministers Kong Guang, Shi Dan and others' deliberations.
translations.idiomatic: I urge Your Majesty to heed the earlier debates of worthy ministers such as Kong Guang and Shi Dan.'

sentences.zh: 議上，遂寢不報。

translations.literal: When deliberation ascended, then lay dormant without reply.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was filed away unanswered.

sentences.zh: 後出為九江太守，定封陵鄉侯。

translations.literal: Later went out as governor of Jiujiang; settled enfeoffment marquis of Lingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: He later served as governor of Jiujiang with a settled fief as Marquis of Lingxiang.

sentences.zh: 統在郡亦有治跡，吏人畏愛之。

translations.literal: Tong in the commandery also had traces of governance; clerks and people feared and loved him.
translations.idiomatic: He governed the commandery well; officials both feared and loved him.

sentences.zh: 卒於官。

translations.literal: Died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office.

sentences.zh: 子松嗣。

translations.literal: Son Song succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liang Song inherited.

sentences.zh: 松字伯孫，少為郎，尚光武女舞陰長公主，再遷虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: Song courtesy name Bosun; in youth was gentleman; married Guangwu's daughter senior princess of Wuyin; twice transferred general of the gentlemen tiger runner.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song, styled Bosun, served young as a gentleman, married Emperor Guangwu's daughter the Princess of Wuyin, and rose to general of the gentlemen tiger runner.

sentences.zh: 松博通經書，明習故事，與諸儒修明堂、辟雍、郊祀、封禪禮儀，常與論議，寵幸莫比。

translations.literal: Song broadly penetrated classics and documents; clearly practiced precedents; with various scholars revised Bright Hall, Border Academy, suburban sacrifice, feng and shan ritual observances; constantly joined debate and deliberation; favor and intimacy none compared.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song mastered the classics and court precedent; he helped the scholars codify Bright Hall, the suburban academy, suburban sacrifice, and the feng and shan rites—none stood closer to the emperor.

sentences.zh: 光武崩，受遺詔輔政。

translations.literal: When Guangwu collapsed, received testament edict assisting governance.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu died, he received orders to assist the regency.

sentences.zh: 永平元年，遷太僕。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongping, transferred to Grand Coachman.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 1 he became Grand Coachman.

sentences.zh: 松數為私書請托郡縣，二年，發覺免官，遂懷怨望。

translations.literal: Song repeatedly made private letters entreating commanderies and counties; in the second year, discovered dismissed office; then harbored resentment and grievance.
translations.idiomatic: He often peddled private requests to local offices; in year 2 he was caught and stripped of rank, and nursed a grudge.

sentences.zh: 四年冬，乃縣飛書誹謗，下獄死，國除。

translations.literal: In winter of the fourth year, then posted flying-letter slander; sent down prison died; state abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of year 4 he circulated slanderous broadsheets, was jailed, died, and lost his marquisate.

sentences.zh: 子扈，後以恭懷皇后從兄，永元中，擢為黃門侍郎，歷位卿、校尉。

translations.literal: Son Hu, later as Empress Gonghuai's elder male cousin, in the Yongyuan period was promoted gentleman of the Yellow Gates; passed through rank minister, colonel.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liang Hu, a cousin of Empress Gonghuai, was raised to gentleman of the Yellow Gates in the Yongyuan era and served as minister and colonel.

sentences.zh: 溫恭謙讓，亦敦《詩》、《書》。

translations.literal: Warm respectful humble yielding; also earnest in Book of Songs and Documents.
translations.idiomatic: He was gentle and modest and steeped in the Songs and Documents.

sentences.zh: 永初中，為長樂少府，松弟竦。

translations.literal: In the Yongchu era he became prefect of Changle; Song's younger brother Song.
translations.idiomatic: During Yongchu Liang Hu served as prefect of Changle. Liang Song the elder's younger brother—also named Liang Song but styled Shujing—is introduced next.

sentences.zh: 竦字叔敬，少習《孟氏易》，弱冠能教授。

translations.literal: Song courtesy name Shujing; in youth studied the Meng family Changes; at capping could teach.
translations.idiomatic: That younger Liang Song, styled Shujing, studied the Meng family's Book of Changes as a youth and could teach at twenty.

sentences.zh: 後坐兄松事，與弟恭俱徙九真。

translations.literal: Later sat for elder brother Song's affair; together with younger brother Gong alike banished to Jiuzhen.
translations.idiomatic: When his brother Liang Song fell, he and his brother Liang Gong were banished to Jiuzhen.

sentences.zh: 既徂南土，歷江、湖，濟沅、湘，感悼子胥、屈原以非辜沈身，乃作《悼騷賦》，系玄石而沈之。

translations.literal: Having gone to southern lands, passed Jiang and lakes, crossed Yuan and Xiang; moved mourned Zixu and Qu Yuan who by non-crime sank bodies; then composed 'Mourning Sao Rhapsody'; tied black stone and sank it.
translations.idiomatic: Southward he crossed the great rivers and grieved for Wu Zixu and Qu Yuan, who drowned though guiltless; he wrote the 'Mourning Sao' and sank it sealed to a stone.

sentences.zh: 顯宗後詔聽還本郡。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaozong afterward edict heard return native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming later allowed him to return home.

sentences.zh: 竦閉門自養，以經籍為娛，著書數篇，名曰《七序》。

translations.literal: Song closed gate self-nurturing; took classics and documents as enjoyment; wrote books several pieces, titled Seven Prefaces.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song lived in retirement with the canon for company and composed several essays gathered as the Seven Prefaces.

sentences.zh: 班固見而稱曰：『孔子著《春秋》而亂臣賊子懼，梁竦作《七序》而竊位素餐者慚。

translations.literal: Ban Gu saw and praised saying: 'Confucius composed Spring and Autumn and traitorous ministers villainous sons feared; Liang Song composed Seven Prefaces and those who steal place eat salary ashamed.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu said, 'Confucius's Spring and Autumn struck fear into traitors; Liang Song's Seven Prefaces shames office-holders who steal pay.'

sentences.zh: 』性好施，不事產業。

translations.literal: ' His nature loved generosity; did not pursue estates.
translations.idiomatic: ' Liang Song loved giving alms and cared nothing for property.'

sentences.zh: 長嫂舞陰公主贍給諸梁，親疏有序，特重敬竦，雖衣食器物，必有加異。

translations.literal: Elder sister-in-law princess of Wuyin supplied various Liangs; close distant had order; specially honored respected Song; though clothes food tools vessels, always had added distinction.
translations.idiomatic: His elder sister-in-law the Princess of Wuyin kept the Liang clan fed by kinship rank but favored Liang Song with finer clothes and goods.

sentences.zh: 竦悉分與親族，自無所服。

translations.literal: Song entirely divided among kin clan; himself had nothing to wear.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song gave it all to kin and dressed plainly himself.

sentences.zh: 竦生長京師，不樂本土，身負其才，郁郁不得意。

translations.literal: Song grew raised capital city; not pleased native land; body bore its talent; depressed not attaining intent.
translations.idiomatic: Raised at court yet restless in Anding, talented Liang Song simmered with frustration.

sentences.zh: 嘗登高遠望，嘆息言曰：『大丈夫居世，生當封侯，死當廟食。

translations.literal: Once climbed high gazed far; sighed breath spoke saying: 'Great husband dwelling age—living ought enfeoff marquis; dying ought temple sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: He climbed a height and sighed, 'A man should win a marquisate in life and offerings in death.'

sentences.zh: 如其不然，閑居可以養誌，《詩》、《書》足以自娛，州郡之職，徒勞人耳。

translations.literal: If it be not so, idle dwelling can nurture aspiration; Book of Songs and Documents sufficient self-amusement; province commandery posts—vainly weary men ears.
translations.idiomatic: If not, retire with the classics for comfort—commandery posts are mere drudgery.'

sentences.zh: 』後辟命交至，並無所就。

translations.literal: ' Later summons orders arrived in succession; together none taken up.
translations.idiomatic: ' Summons came thick and fast—he refused them all.

sentences.zh: 有三男三女，肅宗納其二女，皆為貴人。

translations.literal: He had three sons three daughters; Suzong took in his two daughters; all became honored ladies.
translations.idiomatic: He had three sons and three daughters; Emperor Zhang took two daughters as honored ladies.

sentences.zh: 小貴人生和帝，竇皇后養以為子，而竦家私相慶。

translations.literal: The lesser honored lady bore Emperor He; Empress Dou raised as son; but Song's family privately together rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: The younger honored lady bore Emperor He, whom Empress Dou raised as her own, while the Liang family quietly celebrated.

sentences.zh: 後諸竇聞之，恐梁氏得志，終為己害，建初八年，遂譖殺二貴人，而陷竦等以惡逆。

translations.literal: Later various Dou heard it, feared Liang clan attaining intent, in end for self harm; in eighth year of Jianchu, then slandered killed two honored ladies, and trapped Song and others by heinous rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: The Dou kin, fearing the Liangs' rise, in Jianchu 8 slandered the two honored ladies to death and charged Liang Song and kin with capital treason.

sentences.zh: 詔使漢陽太守鄭據傳考竦罪，死獄中，家屬復徙九真。

translations.literal: Edict caused Hanyang governor Zheng Ju to relay examine Song's crime; died in prison; family dependents again banished Jiuzhen.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered Hanyang governor Zheng Ju to interrogate Liang Song; Song died in jail and his kin were again banished to Jiuzhen.

sentences.zh: 辭語連及舞陰公主，坐徙新城，使者護守。

translations.literal: Words linked reaching princess of Wuyin; sat banished Xincheng; envoys guarded watched.
translations.idiomatic: The indictment touched the Princess of Wuyin; she was banished to Xincheng under guard.

sentences.zh: 宮省事密，莫有知和帝梁氏生者。

translations.literal: Palace omitted affairs secret; none had known Emperor He's Liang clan birth.
translations.idiomatic: Court secrecy hid who had borne Emperor He among the Liangs.

sentences.zh: 永元九年，竇太后崩，松子扈遣從兄禪奏記三府，以為漢家舊典，崇貴母氏，而梁貴人親育聖躬，不蒙尊號，求得申議。

translations.literal: In ninth year of Yongyuan, Empress Dowager Dou collapsed; Song's son Hu sent cousin Shan memorial-record Three Offices, holding Han family's old canons exalted honored mother's clan, yet Liang honored lady personally nurtured sagely body, did not receive honored title; sought obtain extending deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 9 Dowager Dou died; Liang Hu sent his cousin Liang Shan to memorialize the Three Offices that Han tradition honors birth mothers, yet the Liang honored ladies who bore the emperor had received no posthumous dignity—he asked redress.

sentences.zh: 太尉張酺引禪訊問事理，會後召見，因白禪奏記之狀。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhang Pu questioned Liang Shan on the merits of the case; shortly afterward Shan was summoned to audience; Zhang then explained Shan's memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Commandant Zhang Pu questioned Liang Shan on the facts; when the empress received Shan, Zhang explained the memorial.

sentences.zh: 帝感慟良久，曰：『於君意若何？

translations.literal: Emperor moved sorrow long while, said: 'In your lord's intent how?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was moved and asked Zhang Pu, 'What do you think?'

sentences.zh: 』酺對曰：『《春秋》之義，母以子貴。

translations.literal: ' Pu replied saying: 'Spring and Autumn's meaning—mother by son honored.
translations.idiomatic: ' Zhang Pu answered, "The Spring and Autumn teaches that the mother rises with the son."'

sentences.zh: 漢興以來，母氏莫不降顯，臣愚以為宜上尊號，追慰聖靈，存錄諸舅，以明親親。

translations.literal: Since Han's rise, every mother's clan has been elevated to honor—your servant foolishly holds it fitting to ascend honored titles, pursue comforting the sagely spirits, preserve and record the maternal uncles, to clarify loving kin.
translations.idiomatic: Since Han began every birth mother's clan has been honored—grant posthumous titles, comfort their spirits, and ennoble the uncles to show loving kinship.'

sentences.zh: 』帝悲泣曰：『非君孰為朕思之！

translations.literal: ' Emperor wept saying: 'If not you lord who for Us thinks it!
translations.idiomatic: ' The emperor wept, 'Who but you would speak for me!'

sentences.zh: 』會貴人姊南陽樊調妻嫕上書自訟曰：

translations.literal: ' It happened honored lady's elder sister Nanyang Fan Diao's wife Yi submitted memorial self-pleading saying:
translations.idiomatic: ' Meanwhile the honored ladies' elder sister—wife of Fan Diao of Nanyang, née Yi—petitioned in her own defense:'

sentences.zh: 帝覽章感悟，乃下中常侍、掖庭令驗問之，嫕辭證明審，遂得引見，具陳其狀。

translations.literal: Emperor read memorial awakened moved; then sent down palace attendant and commissioner of the imperial harem to verify ask her; Yi's words testimony bright careful; then obtained led audience; fully stated its situation.
translations.idiomatic: Reading the petition, the emperor ordered eunuchs and the harem commissioner to investigate; Lady Yi's testimony held up, and she gained audience to lay out the facts.

sentences.zh: 乃留嫕止宮中，連月乃出，賞賜衣被錢帛第宅奴婢，旬月之間，累資千萬。

translations.literal: Then detained Yi stopping within palace; successive months then emerged; rewarded clothes quilts cash silk mansions slave girls; within ten-day months, accumulated assets ten million.
translations.idiomatic: She stayed in the palace for months and emerged laden with silk, cash, mansions, and servants—her gifts totaled millions within weeks.

sentences.zh: 嫕素有行操，帝益愛之，加號梁夫人；

translations.literal: Yi always had conduct and integrity; emperor increasingly loved her; added title Lady Liang;
translations.idiomatic: Lady Yi was virtuous; the emperor favored her and titled her Lady Liang;

sentences.zh: 擢樊調為羽林左監。

translations.literal: Promoted Fan Diao as left superintendent of the Feathered Forest.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Diao was promoted to left superintendent of the Feathered Forest guard.

sentences.zh: 調，光祿大夫宏兄曾孫也。

translations.literal: Diao was great-grandson of Fan Hong's elder brother; Fan Hong had been superintendent of the imperial household.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Diao came from a collateral line of Fan Hong, who had served as superintendent of the imperial household.

sentences.zh: 於是追尊恭懷皇后。

translations.literal: Thereupon posthumously honored Empress Gonghuai.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Gonghuai was posthumously honored.

sentences.zh: 其冬，制詔三公、大鴻臚曰：『夫孝莫大於尊尊親親，其義一也。

translations.literal: That winter, regulated edict Three Offices, grand herald saying: 'Now filial piety none greater than honoring the honored and cherishing kin—its meaning one.
translations.idiomatic: That winter an edict to the Three Dukes and grand herald read: 'Filial duty chiefly means honoring elders and cherishing kin—the principle is one.'

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「父兮生我，母兮鞠我，撫我畜我，長我育我，顧我復我，出入腹我。

translations.literal: Book of Songs says: "Father oh bore me, mother oh nurtured me, cherish me rear me, grow me raise me, turn to me return me, go out enter shelter me.
translations.idiomatic: The Songs say: "Father begot me, mother nursed me, reared and fed me, watched me come and go, sheltered my going out and in."

sentences.zh: 欲報之德，昊天罔極。

translations.literal: Desiring repay its virtue—vast heaven without limit.
translations.idiomatic: How can I repay such kindness? Heaven is boundless."

sentences.zh: 」朕不敢興事，覽於前世，太宗、中宗，實有舊典，追命外祖，以篤親親。

translations.literal: I dare not lightly undertake new rites; reviewing past reigns, sovereigns set precedent in honoring a mother's parents to strengthen family bonds.
translations.idiomatic: The edict continues: I will not launch novel ceremonies lightly; past emperors left clear models for ennobling a sovereign's maternal kin.

sentences.zh: 其追封謚皇太后父竦為褒親湣侯，比靈文、順成、恩成侯。

translations.literal: Let posthumous rank and title be granted to the father of the Empress Dowager, Liang Song the younger, as Marquis Aiqin Min—rank comparable to the marquises Lingwen, Shuncheng, and Encheng.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song the younger was posthumously invested as Marquis Aiqin Min, in line with earlier honors to empresses' fathers.

sentences.zh: 魂而有靈，嘉斯寵榮，好爵顯服，以慰母心。

translations.literal: If his ghost yet knows, may he rejoice in this glory—fine rank and splendid robes—to comfort his mother's heart.
translations.idiomatic: May his spirit welcome this honor and splendid robes to ease her sorrow.

sentences.zh: 』遣中謁者與嫕及扈，備禮西迎竦喪，詣京師改殯，賜東園畫館、玉匣、衣衾，建塋於恭懷皇后陵傍。

translations.literal: He dispatched palace ushers with Yi and Hu to greet Liang Song's coffin from the west with full rites, bring it to the capital for reburial, and granted an eastern-garden painted inner coffin, jade cases, and quilts; a mound was raised beside Empress Gonghuai's tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Ushers accompanied Yi and Hu west to fetch Liang Song's remains for reburial at court with imperial mortuary gifts beside Empress Gonghuai's mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 帝親臨送葬，百官畢會。

translations.literal: The emperor personally attended the burial; the full bureaucracy assembled.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor escorted the funeral; officials thronged the rites.

sentences.zh: 徵還竦妻、子，封子棠為樂平侯，棠弟雍乘氏侯，雍弟翟單父侯，邑各五千戶，位皆特進，賞賜第宅、奴婢、車馬、兵弩、什物以巨萬計，寵遇光於當世。

translations.literal: Song's wife and children were recalled; son Tang was made Marquis of Leping; Tang's younger brother Yong Marquis of Chengshi; Yong's younger brother Zhai Marquis of Shanfu—five thousand households each, rank specially advanced; mansions, servants, horses, crossbows, and gear worth tens of millions—favor unrivaled in the age.
translations.idiomatic: The family returned: Tang, Yong, and Zhai each gained five-thousand-household marquisates and fortunes beyond reckoning.

sentences.zh: 諸梁內外以親疏並補郎、謁者。

translations.literal: Inside and outside the Liang clan, by kinship distance, all were appointed gentlemen or ushers.
translations.idiomatic: Liang kinsmen near and far filled posts as gentlemen and ushers.

sentences.zh: 棠官至大鴻臚，雍少府。

translations.literal: Tang rose to grand herald; Yong to prefect of the masters of lesser treasury.
translations.idiomatic: Tang became grand herald; Yong superintendent of the masters of lesser treasury.

sentences.zh: 棠卒，子安國嗣，延光中為侍中，有罪免官，諸梁為郎吏者皆坐免。

translations.literal: When Tang died, son Anguo succeeded; in the Yanguang era he was palace attendant, then lost office for crime—all Liangs holding gentleman posts were cashiered.
translations.idiomatic: Anguo succeeded but fell from grace in Yanguang, dragging down every Liang gentleman.

sentences.zh: 商字伯夏，雍之子也。

translations.literal: Shang courtesy name Boxia; Yong's son.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Shang, styled Boxia, was Liang Yong's son.

sentences.zh: 少以外戚拜郎中，遷黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: In youth by imperial kinship appointed gentleman consultant; transferred gentleman of the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: As imperial in-law he rose from gentleman consultant to gentleman of the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 永建元年，襲父封乘氏侯。

translations.literal: In first year of Yongjian inherited father's title Marquis of Chengshi.
translations.idiomatic: Yongjian 1 he inherited the Marquisate of Chengshi.

sentences.zh: 三年，順帝選商女及妹入掖庭，遷侍中、屯騎校尉。

translations.literal: In third year Emperor Shun chose Shang's daughters and younger sisters for the harem; Shang became attendant at the palace gates and colonel of garrison cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Year 3 Emperor Shun enrolled Shang's daughters and sisters; Shang became palace attendant and colonel of garrison cavalry.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉元年，女立為皇后，妹為貴人，加商位特進，更增國土，賜安車駟馬，其歲拜執金吾。

translations.literal: In first year of Yangjia his daughter was established empress and his younger sister honored lady; Shang's rank was specially advanced, his fief enlarged, he received a secure carriage with four horses, and that year became Bearer of the Golden Mace.
translations.idiomatic: Yangjia 1 made his daughter empress; Shang gained rank, land, a ceremonial carriage, and Bearer of the Golden Mace.

sentences.zh: 二年，封子冀為襄邑侯，商讓不受。

translations.literal: Second year: offered son Ji as Marquis of Xiangyi—Shang declined.
translations.idiomatic: Year 2 he refused a marquisate for his son Ji.

sentences.zh: 三年，以商為大將軍，固稱疾不起。

translations.literal: Third year Shang was named Grand General but pleaded illness and would not rise.
translations.idiomatic: Year 3 he was named Grand General but feigned illness.

sentences.zh: 四年，使太常桓焉奉策就第即拜，商乃詣闕受命。

translations.literal: Fourth year the minister of rituals Huan Yan brought the commission to his house; only then did Shang come to the palace to accept.
translations.idiomatic: Year 4 Minister Huan Yan invested him at home—then Shang appeared at court.

sentences.zh: 明年，夫人陰氏薨，追號開封君，贈印綬。

translations.literal: Next year Lady Yin died; posthumously titled lord of Kaifeng with seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Lady Yin died and received posthumous title lord of Kaifeng.

sentences.zh: 商自以戚屬居大位，每存謙柔，虛己進賢，辟漢陽巨覽、上黨陳龜為椽屬。

translations.literal: Though imperial in-law on high office, Shang remained humble, welcomed worthies, and recruited Ju Lan of Hanyang and Chen Gui of Shangdang as staff.
translations.idiomatic: Despite kinship power he stayed modest and recruited Ju Lan and Chen Gui.

sentences.zh: 李固、周舉為從事中郎，於是京師翕然，稱為良輔，帝委重焉。

translations.literal: Li Gu and Zhou Ju served as attendant clerks; the capital praised him as a fine pillar of state—the emperor relied on him.
translations.idiomatic: With Li Gu and Zhou Ju he won fame as a wise minister.

sentences.zh: 每有饑饉，輒載租穀於城門，賑與貧餧，不宣己惠。

translations.literal: Whenever famine struck he loaded grain rental carts at the city gate for the starving without boasting.
translations.idiomatic: In famine he quietly dumped grain rental at the gates for the poor.

sentences.zh: 檢禦門族，未曾以權盛幹法。

translations.literal: He restrained his clan and never let favor bend the law.
translations.idiomatic: He checked his kin and never abused law.

sentences.zh: 而性慎弱無威斷，頗溺於內豎。

translations.literal: Yet he was timid and lacked decisive awe; he indulged eunuchs.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he was weak-willed and yielded to eunuchs.

sentences.zh: 以小黃門曹節等用事於中，遂遣子冀、不疑與為交友，然宦者忌商寵任，反俗陷之。

translations.literal: Junior Yellow Gates Cao Jie and others held sway within; Shang sent sons Ji and Buyi to befriend them—but eunuchs envied his trust and maligned him.
translations.idiomatic: Eunuchs ruled within; Shang's sons befriended them yet jealousy framed him.

sentences.zh: 永和四年，中常侍張逵、蘧政，內者令石光，尚方令傅福，冗從僕射杜永連謀，共譖商及中常侍曹騰、孟賁，雲欲征諸王子，圖議廢立，請收商等案罪。

translations.literal: In fourth year of Yonghe palace attendants Zhang Da, Qu Zheng, Director Shi Guang, Masters of Works Fu Fu, and deputy Yuan Cong plotted together to slander Shang and attendants Cao Teng and Meng Ben—claiming they meant to summon princes and debate deposition—and sought arrest.
translations.idiomatic: Yonghe 4: eunuchs accused Shang and Cao Teng of plotting succession.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：『大將軍父子我所親，騰、賁我所愛，必無是，但汝曹共妒之耳。

translations.literal: The emperor said: 'The Grand General's sons are kin I trust; Teng and Ben I favor—this is mere jealousy.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor dismissed it as envy.

sentences.zh: 』逵等知言不用，懼迫，遂出矯詔收縛騰、賁於省中。

translations.literal: Da and others, seeing their words unused and fearful, forged an edict seizing Teng and Ben in the palace.
translations.idiomatic: They forged orders and seized Cao Teng and Meng Ben.

sentences.zh: 帝聞震怒，敕宦者李歙急呼騰、賁釋之，收逵等，悉伏誅。

translations.literal: The emperor enraged; eunuch Li Xi freed Teng and Ben; Da's party were captured and executed.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor freed Teng and Ben and executed the plotters.

sentences.zh: 辭所連染及在位大臣，商懼多侵枉，乃上疏曰：『《春秋》之義，功在元帥，罪止首惡，故賞不僭溢，刑不淫濫，五帝、三王所以同致康乂也。

translations.literal: Testimony implicated great ministers; Shang feared wrongful ruin and memorialized: 'Spring and Autumn credits the commander but punishes only ringleaders—thus rewards stay measured and punishments never flood—the model of concord.'
translations.idiomatic: Shang urged limiting guilt—the Spring and Autumn rule punishes only ringleaders.

sentences.zh: 竊聞考中常侍張逵等，辭語多所牽及。

translations.literal: He heard examination of Zhang Da's confession dragged many in.
translations.idiomatic: Trials of Zhang Da's confession snared too many.

sentences.zh: 大獄一起，無辜者眾，死囚久系，纖微成大，非所以順迎和氣，平政成化也。

translations.literal: Great inquests strand innocents; long jail kills—this harms harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Mass arrests harm harmony and honest rule.

sentences.zh: 宜早訖竟，以止逮捕之煩。

translations.literal: Close cases quickly to stop endless arrests.
translations.idiomatic: End the dragnet swiftly.

sentences.zh: 』帝乃納之，罪止坐者。

translations.literal: The emperor agreed—punishment stopped at those guilty.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor limited punishment to the guilty.

sentences.zh: 六年秋，商病篤，敕子冀等曰：『吾以不德，享受多福。

translations.literal: Autumn sixth year Shang lay dying; he charged sons Ji: 'My lack of virtue yet drew great fortune.'
translations.idiomatic: Dying in autumn year 6 he told Ji he had enjoyed undeserved favor.

sentences.zh: 生無以輔益朝廷，死必耗廢帑臧，衣衾飯唅玉匣珠貝之屬，何益朽骨。

translations.literal: Living I aided the court little; dying I would waste treasury on jade coffins and pearls—useless to bones.
translations.idiomatic: Extravagant burial wastes the treasury for worthless bones.

sentences.zh: 百僚勞擾，紛華道路，秖增塵垢，雖云禮制，亦有權時。

translations.literal: Officials crowded roads in vain pageantry—only adds dust; ritual may bend to circumstance.
translations.idiomatic: Processions pollute the roads; rites allow expedients.

sentences.zh: 方今邊境不寧，盜賊未息，豈宜重為國損！

translations.literal: Borders restless and bandits active—must not burden the state further!
translations.idiomatic: War and banditry forbid lavish funeral!

sentences.zh: 氣絕之後，載至冢舍，即時殯斂。

translations.literal: After breath stops, carry me to the tomb shed and encoffin at once.
translations.idiomatic: Take me straight to the tomb hall and encoffin immediately.

sentences.zh: 斂以時服，皆以故衣，無更裁制。

translations.literal: Wrap in ordinary clothes—old garments only, no new tailoring.
translations.idiomatic: Shroud in worn garments only.

sentences.zh: 殯已開冢，冢開即葬。

translations.literal: After lying in state open the tomb and bury when opened.
translations.idiomatic: Open the tomb and bury at once after viewing.

sentences.zh: 祭食如存，無用三牲。

translations.literal: Offer food as if living—no three sacrificial beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Simple offerings—no three-animal sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 孝子善述父誌，不宜違我言也。

translations.literal: Filial sons should follow a father's will—do not disobey.
translations.idiomatic: Obey this testament.

sentences.zh: 』及薨，帝親臨喪，諸子欲從其誨，朝廷不聽，賜以東園朱壽器、銀鏤、黃腸、玉匣、什物二十八種，錢二百萬，布三千匹。

translations.literal: When he died the emperor attended mourning; sons wished compliance but court refused—granted eastern-garden red lacquer coffin, silver inlay, yellow-heart timber, jade case, twenty-eight kinds of gear, two million cash, three thousand bolts cloth.
translations.idiomatic: The court overruled plain burial with imperial mortuary gifts.

sentences.zh: 皇后錢五百萬，布萬匹。

translations.literal: The empress gave five million cash and ten thousand bolts.
translations.idiomatic: The empress added cash and silk.

sentences.zh: 及葬，贈輕車介士，賜謚忠侯。

translations.literal: Burial brought light chariot escort and posthumous title Marquis Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: He received escort and the posthumous title Loyal Marquis.

sentences.zh: 中宮親送，帝幸宣陽亭，瞻望車騎。

translations.literal: The empress dowager escorted; emperor watched from Xuanyang pavilion.
translations.idiomatic: The court escorted; the emperor watched from Xuanyang.

sentences.zh: 子冀嗣。

translations.literal: Son Ji succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji succeeded.

sentences.zh: 冀字伯卓。

translations.literal: Ji courtesy name Bozhuo.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji, styled Bozhuo.

sentences.zh: 為人鳶肩豺目，洞精目黨眄，口吟舌言，裁能書計。

translations.literal: He had kite shoulders, jackal eyes, sunken gleaming pupils looking sideways, stammered tongue—barely literate.
translations.idiomatic: Grotesque features, sidelong glare, stammer—barely able to read.

sentences.zh: 少為貴戚，逸遊自恣。

translations.literal: Young noble idler—debauchery unrestrained.
translations.idiomatic: A privileged youth ran wild.

sentences.zh: 性嗜酒，能挽滿、彈棋、格五、六博、蹴鞠、意錢之戲，又好臂鷹走狗，騁馬鬥雞。

translations.literal: Drunkard fond of drinking games, chess, dice, ball, cockfight—hawking and dogs.
translations.idiomatic: He gambled, drank, hunted, and rode.

sentences.zh: 初為黃門侍郎，轉侍中、虎賁中郎將，越騎、步兵校尉，執金吾。

translations.literal: First gentleman of Yellow Gates; then attendant, tiger-runner general, colonels of assault infantry and Bearer of Golden Mace.
translations.idiomatic: He climbed through palace guards to Bearer of the Golden Mace.

sentences.zh: 永和元年，拜河南尹。

translations.literal: Yonghe first year named governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Yonghe 1 he governed Henan.

sentences.zh: 冀居職暴恣，多非法，父商所親客洛陽令呂放，頗與商言及冀之短，商以讓冀，冀即遣人於道刺殺放。

translations.literal: Ji ruled Henan with cruelty; Luoyang magistrate Lü Fang, father's client, told Shang Ji's faults—Shang rebuked Ji; Ji had Fang assassinated on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Ji murdered magistrate Lü Fang who exposed him.

sentences.zh: 而恐商知之，乃推疑於放之怨仇，請以放弟禹為洛陽令，使捕之，盡滅其宗親、賓客百餘人。

translations.literal: Fearing Shang's knowledge he blamed Fang's enemies, asked Fang's brother Yu as Luoyang magistrate to arrest them—wiping out over a hundred kin and clients.
translations.idiomatic: He framed rivals and exterminated over a hundred innocents.

sentences.zh: 商薨未及葬，順帝乃拜冀為大將軍，弟侍中不疑為河南尹。

translations.literal: Before Shang was buried Shangdi named Ji Grand General and younger brother Buyi governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Before the funeral ended Ji became Grand General and Buyi governor of Henan.

sentences.zh: 及帝崩，沖帝始在繈褓，太后臨朝，詔冀與太傅趙峻、太尉李固參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: When emperor died Emperor Chong was infant; empress dowager ruled—ordered Ji with tutors Zhao Jun and Li Gu supervise Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Regency placed Ji with Zhao Jun and Li Gu over the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 冀雖辭不肯當，而侈暴滋甚。

translations.literal: Though Ji declined office his arrogance grew.
translations.idiomatic: Ji feigned refusal while growing worse.

sentences.zh: 沖帝又崩，冀立質帝。

translations.literal: When Chong died Ji set up Emperor Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Chong died; Ji enthroned Emperor Zhi.

sentences.zh: 帝少而聰慧，知冀驕橫，嘗朝群臣，目冀曰：『此跋扈將軍也。

translations.literal: The boy was clever; knowing Ji's pride at court he called Ji 'that domineering general.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhi called him the domineering general.

sentences.zh: 』冀聞，深惡之，遂令左右進鴆加煮餅，帝即日崩。

translations.literal: Ji hated it—had attendants poison boiled cakes; emperor died same day.
translations.idiomatic: Ji poisoned him with cakes that day.

sentences.zh: 復立桓帝，而枉害李固及前太尉杜喬，海內嗟懼，語在《李固傳》。

translations.literal: Ji enthroned Emperor Huan and murdered Li Gu and Du Qiao—told in Li Gu's biography.
translations.idiomatic: He raised Huan and killed Li Gu and Du Qiao—see Li Gu's memoir.

sentences.zh: 建和元年，益封冀萬三千戶，增大將軍府舉高第茂才，官屬倍於三公。

translations.literal: Jianhe 1 added thirteen thousand households to Ji, enlarged grand general bureau to nominate top graduates—staff doubled the Three Dukes.
translations.idiomatic: Jianhe 1 swelled his fief and bureau beyond the Three Dukes.

sentences.zh: 又封不疑為潁陽侯，不疑弟蒙西平侯，冀子胤襄邑侯，各萬戶。

translations.literal: Buyi made Marquis of Yingyang; Meng Marquis of Xiping; son Yin Marquis of Xiangyi—ten thousand each.
translations.idiomatic: Kin gained ten-thousand-household marquisates.

sentences.zh: 和平元年，重增封冀萬戶，並前所襲合三萬戶。

translations.literal: Heping 1 another ten thousand—three ten thousand total.
translations.idiomatic: Heping 1 brought his holdings to thirty thousand households.

sentences.zh: 弘農人宰宣素性佞邪，欲取媚於冀，乃上言大將軍有周公之功，今既封諸子，則其妻宜為邑君。

translations.literal: Hongnong flatterer Zai Xuan praised Ji as Duke of Zhou—said Ji's wife should be lady of a domain.
translations.idiomatic: Zai Xuan flattered Ji as Duke of Zhou and urged honoring his wife.

sentences.zh: 詔遂封冀妻孫壽為襄城君，兼食陽翟租，歲入五千萬，加賜赤紱，比長公主。

translations.literal: Edict made wife Sun Shou Lady of Xiangcheng with Yangdi revenue—fifty million yearly income, crimson ribbon like senior princess.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shou gained Lady of Xiangcheng with vast rent and princess rank.

sentences.zh: 壽色美而善為妖態，作愁眉，啼妝，墮馬髻，折腰步，齲齒笑，以為媚惑。

translations.literal: Shou was beautiful and theatrical—knit brows, tear makeup, fallen-horse coiffure, broken-waist gait, tooth-gap smile—all seduction.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shou invented fashions of seduction.

sentences.zh: 冀亦改易輿服之制，作平上軿車，埤幘，狹冠，折上巾，擁身扇，狐尾單衣。

translations.literal: Ji altered carriage dress—flat-topped light carriage, low kerchief, narrow hat, folded kerchief, body fan, fox-tail robe.
translations.idiomatic: Ji sported outlandish clothing and carriages.

sentences.zh: 壽性鉗忌，能制禦冀，冀甚寵憚之。

translations.literal: Shou was jealous and controlled Ji—Ji both doting and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shou dominated Ji through jealousy.

sentences.zh: 冀用壽言，多斥奪諸梁在位者，外以謙讓，而實崇孫氏宗親。

translations.literal: Following Shou, Ji stripped Liangs of office outwardly humble while promoting Sun kin.
translations.idiomatic: He ousted Liangs and elevated Suns.

sentences.zh: 冒名而為侍中、卿、校尉、郡守、長吏者十餘人，皆貪叨凶淫，各遣私客籍屬縣富人，被以它罪，閉獄掠拷，使出錢自贖，資物少者至於死徙。

translations.literal: Over ten Suns held court posts—greedy and cruel—agents marked rich counties, framed them, jailed and extorted ransom—small sums meant death or exile.
translations.idiomatic: Sun placemen extorted counties by false charges.

sentences.zh: 扶風人士孫奮居富而性吝，冀因以馬乘遺之，從貸錢五千萬，奮以三千萬與之，冀大怒，乃告郡縣，認奮母為其守臧婢，雲盜白珠十斛、紫金千斤以叛，遂收考奮兄弟，死於獄中，悉沒資財億七千餘萬。

translations.literal: Wealthy miser Shifu Fen of Fufeng—Ji lent horses demanding fifty million; Fen gave thirty—Ji accused Fen's mother as stolen slave girl with phantom gems—executed Fen brothers and seized seven hundred million assets.
translations.idiomatic: Ji ruined rich Shifu Fen on a trumped theft.

sentences.zh: 其四方調發，歲時貢獻，皆先輸上第於冀，乘輿乃其次焉。

translations.literal: Regional tribute went first to Ji—imperial share second.
translations.idiomatic: Provincial tribute fed Ji before the throne.

sentences.zh: 吏人賫貨求官請罪者，道路相望。

translations.literal: Officials bearing bribes for posts clogged roads.
translations.idiomatic: Buyers of office crowded the roads.

sentences.zh: 冀又遣客出塞，交通外國，廣求異物。

translations.literal: Ji sent agents beyond the frontier for exotic goods.
translations.idiomatic: He trafficked abroad for curiosities.

sentences.zh: 因行道路，發取伎女禦者，而使人復乘勢橫暴，妻略婦女，歐擊吏卒，所在怨毒。

translations.literal: On the road they seized singing girls and drivers—agents raped women and beat guards—hatred everywhere.
translations.idiomatic: His men seized women and abused folk along routes.

sentences.zh: 冀乃大起第舍，而壽亦對街為宅，殫極土木，互相誇競。

translations.literal: Ji raised mansions; Shou built opposite—extravagant rivalry.
translations.idiomatic: Husband and wife rivaled in palace-building.

sentences.zh: 堂寢皆有陰陽奧室，連房洞戶。

translations.literal: Halls and chambers had yin-yang suites—linked rooms and tunnel doors.
translations.idiomatic: Rooms twisted with hidden passages.

sentences.zh: 柱壁雕鏤，加以銅漆，窗牖皆有綺疏青瑣，圖以雲氣仙靈。

translations.literal: Carved pillars lacquered bronze—windows with silk lattice and green bolt patterns painted with clouds and immortals.
translations.idiomatic: Bronzed carved halls with immortal frescoes.

sentences.zh: 臺閣周通，更相臨望；

translations.literal: Terraces linked—mutual viewing.
translations.idiomatic: Linked terraces overlooked each other.

sentences.zh: 飛梁石蹬，陵跨水道。

translations.literal: Flying bridges and stone steps crossed waterways.
translations.idiomatic: Stone bridges vaulted streams.

sentences.zh: 金玉珠璣，異方珍怪，充積臧室。

translations.literal: Gold jade pearls odd treasures filled vaults.
translations.idiomatic: Treasure chambers overflowed.

sentences.zh: 遠致汗血名馬。

translations.literal: Imported sweat-blood horses.
translations.idiomatic: He imported famed horses.

sentences.zh: 又廣開園囿，采土築山，十里九陂，以像二崤，深林絕澗，有若自然，奇禽馴獸，飛走其間。

translations.literal: He dug parks—earth hills ten li nine slopes mimicking Two Xiao—deep woods gorges like nature—exotic birds beasts roaming.
translations.idiomatic: He sculpted miniature mountains and stocked exotic game.

sentences.zh: 冀、壽共乘輦車，張羽蓋，飾以金銀，遊觀第內，多從倡伎，鳴鐘吹管，酣謳竟路。

translations.literal: Ji and Shou rode palanquin with feather canopy silver gold—musicians and bells touring grounds.
translations.idiomatic: They rode in splendor with musicians.

sentences.zh: 或連繼日夜，以騁娛恣。

translations.literal: Sometimes day and night parties.
translations.idiomatic: Debauchery ran day and night.

sentences.zh: 客到門不得通，皆請謝門者，門者累千金。

translations.literal: Guests paid gatekeepers thousands for entry.
translations.idiomatic: Gatekeepers grew rich on bribes.

sentences.zh: 又多拓林苑，禁同王家，西至弘農，東界滎陽，南極魯陽，北達河、淇，包含山藪，遠帶丘荒，周旋封域，殆將千里。

translations.literal: Parks rivaled royal bounds—west Hongnong east Yingyang south Luyang north River Qi—nearly thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: His hunting grounds spanned almost a thousand li.

sentences.zh: 又起菟苑於河南城西，經亙數十里，發屬縣卒徒，繕修樓觀，數年乃成。

translations.literal: Built Rabbit Park west of Henan—tens of li—forced labor years.
translations.idiomatic: He built a vast Rabbit Park west of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 移檄所在，調發生菟，刻其毛以為識，人有犯者，罪至刑死。

translations.literal: Orders demanded live rabbits—marked fur—killers faced death.
translations.idiomatic: Marked rabbits—death for killing one.

sentences.zh: 嘗有西域賈胡，不知禁忌，誤殺一兔，轉相告言，坐死者十餘人。

translations.literal: Western merchant unknowingly killed one rabbit—ten executed in chain reaction.
translations.idiomatic: A foreign merchant accidentally killed a rabbit—over ten died.

sentences.zh: 冀二弟嘗私遣人出獵上黨，冀聞而捕其賓客，一時殺三十餘人，無生還者。

translations.literal: Ji's brothers hunted Shangdang—Ji seized their guests and killed thirty none survived.
translations.idiomatic: Ji slaughtered thirty guests of hunting brothers.

sentences.zh: 冀又起別第於城西，以納奸亡。

translations.literal: Ji built western villa hiding fugitives.
translations.idiomatic: He hid fugitives in a western compound.

sentences.zh: 或取良人，悉為奴婢，至數千人，名曰『自賣人』。

translations.literal: Kidnapped commoners as slaves—thousands called self-sellers.
translations.idiomatic: He seized thousands as slaves labeled self-sellers.

sentences.zh: 時，郎中汝南袁著，年十九，見冀凶縱，不勝其憤，乃詣闕上書曰：

translations.literal: Gentleman Yuan Zhu of Runan, nineteen, seeing Ji's tyranny, memorialized at palace gate:
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Zhu at nineteen attacked Ji's crimes.

sentences.zh: 臣聞仲尼嘆鳳鳥不至，河不出圖，自傷卑賤，不能致也。

translations.literal: Your servant heard Confucius sighed phoenix and river chart absent—blaming low station.
translations.idiomatic: He cites Confucius longing for omens.

sentences.zh: 今陛下居得致之位，又有能致之資，而和氣未應，賢愚失序者，勢分權臣，上下壅隔之故也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty holds rank and means yet harmony lacks—because mighty ministers block high and low.
translations.idiomatic: Powerful ministers isolate the throne.

sentences.zh: 夫四時之運，功成則退，高爵厚寵，鮮不致災。

translations.literal: Seasons retire when done—great reward invites disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Seasons turn; peak power invites ruin.

sentences.zh: 今大將軍位極功成，可為至戒，宜遵懸車之禮，高枕頤神。

translations.literal: Grand General at zenith should retire—suspend carriage rite—pillow ease.
translations.idiomatic: Ji should yield power like hanging up carriage.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「木實繁者，披枝害心。

translations.literal: Tradition says: "When a tree bears abundant fruit, spreading branches wound its core.
translations.idiomatic: The adage runs: heavy fruit breaks the branches.

sentences.zh: 」若不抑損權盛，將無以全其身矣。

translations.literal: " If soaring power is not curbed, one cannot save oneself.
translations.idiomatic: Unless might is checked, no man stays whole.

sentences.zh: 左右聞臣言，將側目切齒，臣特以童蒙見拔，故敢忘忌諱。

translations.literal: Those at court who hear me will glower and grind their teeth; I was promoted while still a mere youth, so I dare break silence.
translations.idiomatic: His Majesty's attendants will hate this—I was plucked young from obscurity, which is why I risk taboo.

sentences.zh: 昔舜、禹相戒無若丹朱，周公戒成王無如殷王紂，願除誹謗之罪，以開天下之口。

translations.literal: Once Shun and Yu warned one another not to be like Dan Zhu; the Duke of Zhou warned King Cheng not to be like King Zhou of Yin. Abolish the law against slander and open every mouth under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: He cites ancient warnings against wicked kings and begs repeal of the anti-slander statutes so truth can be spoken.

sentences.zh: 書得奏御，冀聞而密遣掩捕著。

translations.literal: When the memorial reached the throne, Ji heard and secretly sent men to seize Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji learned of the memorial and had Yuan Zhu arrested in secret.

sentences.zh: 著乃變易姓名，後託病偽死，結蒲為人，市棺殯送。

translations.literal: Zhu changed his name, feigned death from illness, fashioned a rush dummy, bought a coffin, and staged a funeral.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Zhu went underground—fake corpse in a coffin—and vanished.

sentences.zh: 冀廉問知其詐，陰求得，笞殺之，隱蔽其事。

translations.literal: Ji investigated, learned the fraud, tracked Zhu down, beat him to death, and hushed the matter.
translations.idiomatic: Ji uncovered the ruse, had Yuan Zhu flogged to death, and buried the story.

sentences.zh: 學生桂陽劉常，當世名儒，素善於著，冀召補令史以辱之。

translations.literal: His pupil Liu Chang of Guiyang, a famous scholar, had long been close to Zhu; Ji appointed him chief clerk to humiliate him.
translations.idiomatic: Ji named the eminent Liu Chang recorder under him to shame both men.

sentences.zh: 時，太原郝絜、胡武，皆危言高論，與著友善。

translations.literal: At the time Hao Jie and Hu Wu of Taiyuan spoke bold, lofty words and were friends with Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Taiyuan scholars Hao Jie and Hu Wu were Yuan Zhu's allies in outspoken protest.

sentences.zh: 先是，絜等連名奏記三府，薦海內高士，而不詣冀，冀追怒之，又疑為著黨，敕中部官移檄捕前奏記者並殺之，遂誅武家，死者六十餘人。

translations.literal: Earlier Jie and others had jointly memorialized the Three Offices recommending worthies of the realm but had not called on Ji; Ji pursued them in fury, also suspecting them Zhu's faction—ordered capital officers circulate warrants, seized prior memorialists and killed them, then exterminated Wu's household—over sixty dead.
translations.idiomatic: After Hao Jie's coalition bypassed Liang Ji, Ji accused them of faction, executed dozens of signatories, and wiped out Hu Wu's clan.

sentences.zh: 絜初逃亡，知不得免，因輿櫬奏書冀門。

translations.literal: Jie fled at first; knowing he could not escape, he had his corpse-cart driven to Ji's gate with a final memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Hao Jie, seeing no escape, delivered his own death warrant to Liang Ji's door.

sentences.zh: 書入，仰藥而死，家乃得全。

translations.literal: When the memorial was delivered he drank poison and died; his family was spared.
translations.idiomatic: He took poison once the letter arrived—his kin survived.

sentences.zh: 及冀誅，有詔以禮祀著等。

translations.literal: When Ji was executed an edict ordered sacrifice to Zhu and the others by proper rite.
translations.idiomatic: After Ji fell, the court honored Yuan Zhu posthumously.

sentences.zh: 冀諸忍忌，皆此類也。

translations.literal: Ji's cruelties and jealousies were all of this sort.
translations.idiomatic: Such was Liang Ji's habitual malice.

sentences.zh: 不疑好經書，善待士，冀陰疾之，因中常侍白帝，轉為光祿勛，又諷眾人共薦其子胤為河南尹。

translations.literal: Buyi loved the classics and treated scholars well; Ji secretly hated him and through a palace attendant persuaded the emperor to transfer him to superintendent of the palace guards, while also inducing the court to recommend his son Yin as governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Ji envied Liang Buyi's virtue—demoted him to a nominal guard post while forcing Yin into Henan.

sentences.zh: 胤一名胡狗，時年十六，容貌甚陋，不勝冠帶，道路見者，莫不蚩笑焉。

translations.literal: Yin was also called Hu Gou; at sixteen he was very ugly and could hardly wear cap and sash—passersby laughed openly.
translations.idiomatic: The boy nicknamed Dog was grotesque at sixteen—people mocked him in the street.

sentences.zh: 不疑自恥兄弟有隙，遂讓位歸第，與弟蒙閉門自守。

translations.literal: Buyi was ashamed that brothers were estranged; he yielded his office and returned home, closing gates with Meng to live quietly.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Buyi quit office and shut himself in with Liang Meng.

sentences.zh: 冀不欲令與賓客交通，陰使人變服至門，記往來者。

translations.literal: Ji did not want him meeting guests; secretly he sent men in disguise to the gate to log every visitor.
translations.idiomatic: Ji spied on Buyi's visitors with disguised watchers.

sentences.zh: 南郡太守馬融、江夏太守田明，初除，過謁不疑，冀諷州郡以它事陷之，皆髡笞徙朔方。

translations.literal: Governors Ma Rong of Nan and Tian Ming of Jiangxia, newly appointed, paid respects to Buyi; Ji induced commanderies to trap them on other charges—both shaved, flogged, and exiled north.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong and Tian Ming called on Buyi; Ji framed them for exile and mutilation.

sentences.zh: 融自刺不誅，明遂死於路。

translations.literal: Rong stabbed himself but was not killed; Ming died on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong survived self-stabbing; Tian Ming perished en route.

sentences.zh: 永興二年，封不疑子馬為潁陰侯，胤子桃為城父侯。

translations.literal: In second year of Yongxing Buyi's son Ma was made Marquis of Yingyin and Yin's son Tao Marquis of Chengfu.
translations.idiomatic: Yongxing 2 brought marquisates to Ma and Tao.

sentences.zh: 冀一門前後七封侯，三皇后，六貴人，二大將軍，夫人、女食邑稱君者七人，尚公主者三人，其餘卿、將、尹、校五十七人。

translations.literal: The single clan held seven marquisates in succession, three empresses, six honored ladies, two grand generals; seven women with titled domains as ladies, three who married princesses; fifty-seven others ministers, generals, prefects, and colonels.
translations.idiomatic: One Liang line counted seven marquises, three empresses, six consorts, two grand generals, and dozens of high posts.

sentences.zh: 在位二十餘年，究極滿盛，威行內外，百僚側目，莫敢違命，天子恭己而不得有所親豫。

translations.literal: They held power over twenty years, reached utmost fullness; awe ran inside and out—officials sidelong glanced—none dared disobey—the emperor kept ritual reverence and could decide nothing himself.
translations.idiomatic: For twenty years the Liangs terrified the bureaucracy while the emperor signed whatever they wished.

sentences.zh: 初，掖庭人鄧香妻宣生女猛，香卒，宣更適梁紀。

translations.literal: Earlier harem woman Deng Xiang's wife Xuan bore daughter Meng; when Xiang died Xuan remarried a man named Liang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Meng was Deng Xiang's daughter by Lady Xuan; after Xiang died Xuan married Liang Ji, who stood in an uncle's relation to Sun Shou.

sentences.zh: 梁紀者，冀妻壽之舅也。

translations.literal: That Liang Ji was maternal uncle to Ji's wife Sun Shou.
translations.idiomatic: That same Liang Ji was uncle to Sun Shou on her mother's side.

sentences.zh: 壽引進猛入掖庭，見幸，為貴人，冀因欲認猛為其女以自固，乃易猛姓為梁。

translations.literal: Shou introduced Meng into the harem; she won favor as honored lady; Ji wished to claim her as his daughter to secure himself—therefore changed Meng's surname to Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shou installed Deng Meng at court; Liang Ji adopted her as a daughter and renamed her Liang.

sentences.zh: 時猛姊婿邴尊為議郎，冀恐尊沮敗宣意，乃結刺客於偃城，刺殺尊，而又欲殺宣。

translations.literal: At the time Meng's brother-in-law Bing Zun was consultant; Ji feared Zun would thwart Xuan's plans—therefore stationed assassins at Yancheng who killed Zun, and also wished to kill Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Ji murdered Bing Zun to silence Deng Meng's kin and meant to kill Lady Xuan.

sentences.zh: 宣家在延熹裏，與中常侍袁赦相比，冀使刺客登赦屋，欲入宣家。

translations.literal: Xuan's house lay in Yanxi ward beside palace attendant Yuan She; Ji sent assassins onto She's roof to reach Xuan's home.
translations.idiomatic: Assault teams climbed Yuan She's roof to strike Lady Xuan next door.

sentences.zh: 赦覺之，鳴鼓會眾以告宣。

translations.literal: She perceived it—beat drums and gathered a crowd to warn Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan She raised the alarm and warned Lady Xuan.

sentences.zh: 宣馳入以白帝，帝大怒，遂與中常侍單超、具瑗、唐衡、左悺、徐璜等五人成謀誅冀。

translations.literal: Xuan galloped to report to the emperor; the emperor was greatly angered and with palace attendants Shan Chao, Ju Yuan, Tang Heng, Zuo Guan, and Xu Huang plotted Ji's execution.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Xuan reached Emperor Huan; he allied with the five eunuchs to destroy Liang Ji.

sentences.zh: 語在《宦者傳》。

translations.literal: The story is told in the Eunuchs memoir.
translations.idiomatic: Details appear in the biography of the eunuchs.

sentences.zh: 冀心疑超等，乃使中黃門張惲入省宿，以防其變。

translations.literal: Ji suspected Chao and the others; therefore sent palace attendant Zhang Yun into the palace quarters overnight to guard against a coup.
translations.idiomatic: Ji posted Zhang Yun inside the palace as a precaution.

sentences.zh: 具瑗敕吏收惲，以輒從外入，欲圖不軌。

translations.literal: Ju Yuan ordered clerks seize Yun—charging that he had entered from outside without authority intending treason.
translations.idiomatic: Ju Yuan arrested Zhang Yun on a forged charge of intrusion.

sentences.zh: 帝因是御前殿，召諸尚書入，發其事，使尚書令尹勛持節勒丞郎以下皆操兵守省閣，斂諸符節送省中。

translations.literal: The emperor therefore ascended the front hall, summoned the Masters of Writing, exposed the affair, ordered Yin Xun with staff to compel assistants downward—all bearing arms guarding palace gates—and gathered tally credentials to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan seized the palace offices, armed the clerks, and collected imperial tallies.

sentences.zh: 使黃門令具瑗將左右廄騶、虎賁、羽林、都候斂戟士，合千餘人，與司隸校尉張彪共圍冀第。

translations.literal: He ordered Huangmen director Ju Yuan to lead stable grooms, tiger runners, feather forest guards, and capital halberdiers—over a thousand men—with metropolitan commander Zhang Biao to surround Ji's residence.
translations.idiomatic: Over a thousand guards encircled Liang Ji's mansion under Zhang Biao.

sentences.zh: 使光祿勛袁盱持節收冀大將軍印綬，徙封比景都鄉侯。

translations.literal: He ordered superintendent Yuan Xu with staff to take Ji's grand general seal and ribbon and transfer his fief to village marquis of Bijing.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Xu stripped Liang Ji of command and demoted him to a village marquis at Bijing.

sentences.zh: 冀及妻壽即日皆自殺。

translations.literal: Ji and wife Shou that same day both committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji and Sun Shou killed themselves the same day.

sentences.zh: 悉收子河南尹胤、叔父屯騎校尉讓，及親從衛尉淑、越騎校尉忠、長水校尉戟等，諸梁及孫氏中外宗親送詔獄，無長少皆棄市。

translations.literal: All seized—son Yin governor of Henan, uncle colonel Rang, kin Su, Zhong, Ji the halberdier—every Liang and Sun inside and outside kin sent to edict prison—all executed in market square young and old alike.
translations.idiomatic: The purge swept Liang and Sun kin—none spared the scaffold.

sentences.zh: 不疑、蒙先卒。

translations.literal: Buyi and Meng had died earlier.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Buyi and Liang Meng were already dead.

sentences.zh: 其它所連及公卿、列校、尉刺史、二千石死者數十人，故吏賓客免黜者三百餘人，朝廷為空，惟尹勛、袁盱及廷尉邯鄲義在焉。

translations.literal: Others implicated—dozens of ministers, colonels, prefects, two-thousand-shi officials dead; three hundred former clients dismissed—the court stood empty save Yin Xun, Yuan Xu, and commandant Handan Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Scores of officials fell; only Yin Xun, Yuan Xu, and Handan Yi remained at their posts.

sentences.zh: 是時事卒從中發，使者交馳，公卿失其度，官府市里鼎沸，數日乃定，百姓莫不稱慶。

translations.literal: The coup erupted suddenly—messengers galloped—officials lost composure—capital markets boiled—days passed before calm—commoners everywhere rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: Luoyang churned for days until calm returned—the people celebrated.

sentences.zh: 收冀財貨，縣官斥賣，合三十餘萬萬，以充王府，用減天下稅租之半。

translations.literal: Ji's property seized—state auctioned—totaled three hundred thousand million cash—to fill the royal treasury—halving the empire's land tax.
translations.idiomatic: Liquidating his hoard yielded billions for the treasury and cut rents by half.

sentences.zh: 散其苑囿，以業窮民。

translations.literal: His parks were broken up and given to the destitute for livelihood.
translations.idiomatic: Royal hunting grounds became farms for the poor.

sentences.zh: 錄誅冀功者，封尚書令尹勛以下數十人。

translations.literal: Merit in killing Ji rewarded—Master of Writing Yin Xun downward dozens enfeoffed.
translations.idiomatic: Dozens who aided the coup received titles.

sentences.zh: 論曰：順帝之世，梁商稱為賢輔，豈以其地居亢滿，而能以願謹自終者乎？

translations.literal: The appraisal says: In Emperor Shun's reign Liang Shang was called a worthy minister—was it because though standing at the brim of fullness he managed cautious self-restraint to the end?
translations.idiomatic: Why was Liang Shang praised—because he stayed humble atop perilous height?

sentences.zh: 夫宰相運動樞極，感會天人，中於道則易以興政，乖於務則難乎禦物。

translations.literal: The chancellor turns the pivot—links heaven and humanity—when centered on the Way government rises easily; when tasks diverge things prove hard to manage.
translations.idiomatic: High ministers harmonize heaven and earth—or lose control when they stray.

sentences.zh: 商協回天之勢，屬雕弱之期，而匡朝恤患，未聞上述，憔悴之音，載謠人口。

translations.literal: Shang possessed power to reverse heaven—during a weak age yet aided court and pitied calamity—superior counsel was not heard—worn lament traveled on every tongue.
translations.idiomatic: Shang could have steered the age yet famine songs spread unchecked.

sentences.zh: 雖輿粟盈門，何救阻饑之厄；

translations.literal: Though grain carts jammed the gates—how could that relieve famine's scourge;
translations.idiomatic: Grain handouts at the gate never ended famine;

sentences.zh: 永言終制，未解屍官之尤。

translations.literal: His testament urged humble burial—yet did not lift blame for officials stripped of corpse guardianship.
translations.idiomatic: His humble funeral vow never cleared colleagues cashiered with him.

sentences.zh: 況乃傾側孽臣，傳寵凶嗣，以致破家傷國，而豈徒然哉！

translations.literal: How much more when favored ministers become villainous heirs—destroying clan and state—not without cause!
translations.idiomatic: Far worse to breed Liang Ji—ruin follows wicked favor.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：在河西佐漢，統亦定算。

translations.literal: The verse says: west of the river they aided Han—Tong too fixed the plan.
translations.idiomatic: Verse: Hexi backed Han restoration; Liang Tong planned it.

sentences.zh: 褒親幽憤，升高累嘆。

translations.literal: Honoring kin—gloomy grievance—rising high draws piled sighs.
translations.idiomatic: Maternal honors brought sorrow; ambition climbed to ruin.

sentences.zh: 商恨善柔。

translations.literal: Shang regretted softness.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Shang paid for gentleness.

sentences.zh: 冀遂貪亂。

translations.literal: Ji followed greed into chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji rushed greed to catastrophe.
